steps.title: Identify the sentence's main verb
steps.content:
Strip the sentence to its core: A plano-convex lens ______ the laser... ensured a spot size of 85 micrometers.

The main verb of the subject lens is ensured. So the blank cannot be a second main verb — it must form a modifier describing the lens.

steps.title: Use the participial modifier
steps.content:
A finite verb (focused, focuses, focus) would create two main verbs with no conjunction — a comma splice / run-on.

The slot needs a participial phrase that modifies lens: A plano-convex lens focusing the laser on the center of the crystal ensured a spot size of 85 micrometers.

Only focusing works as a participle.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
